title: T-106 — surface EvaluateResult through ExecutorResult if executeRule is ever made async (FT-046)
prompt:
# T-106 — surface EvaluateResult through ExecutorResult if executeRule is ever made async (FT-046)

PRD: `docs/prd/00-FOUNDATION-PRD.md` — sections: §7
Project root: `/Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira`
Original carry-forward ID: **FT-046** (from `_meta/audits/2026-05-07-foundation-final-carry-forward-backlog.md`)
Severity: **P3** (M effort)

## Goal
ExecutorResult exposes optional async_result field; test demonstrates wiring path.

## Implementation contract
1. ExecutorResult adds `async_result?: EvaluateResult`.
2. Test stubs an async-rule path that populates the field and asserts it round-trips.

## Files (scope)
- packages/executor/src/result-types.ts [modify]
- packages/executor/src/__tests__/async-result.test.ts [create]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given an async-rule stub, when dispatched, then ExecutorResult.async_result is populated with the EvaluateResult.
2. Given a sync-rule, when dispatched, then ExecutorResult.async_result is undefined.

## Test strategy
- RED: write async-result.test.ts asserting both branches before type change.
- Run `pnpm --filter @monopilot/executor test async-result`.

## Risk red lines
- Do not introduce a breaking change to existing sync consumers.

## Closeout evidence
Provide: changed_files, test_commands_and_results, acceptance_criteria_status (per AC), deviations_from_prd, git_status. Use the test-driven-development skill (RED-first) and requesting-code-review at GREEN.

pipeline_inputs.description: When executeRule becomes async (future), surface the full EvaluateResult through ExecutorResult so consumers can see all rule outputs.
pipeline_inputs.details: Speculative-future: today executeRule is sync and ExecutorResult collapses the structure. Add an `async_result?: EvaluateResult` field that future async-executeRule paths can populate.
out_of_scope: Do not implement async executeRule itself.

Given a sync-rule, when dispatched, then ExecutorResult.async_result is undefined.
